# Auto-generated by Copilot Agent | Repo: Cortex Second Brain
# Regenerate with: paste sprint prompt template into Copilot Agent chat

## Project Overview

Cortex Second Brain is an AI-powered, offline-capable Progressive Web App (PWA) for personal knowledge management. Users capture notes, documents, and web content into a searchable knowledge base and interact with **TESSA**, an AI assistant backed by OpenAI, to surface insights across their saved content. The project is in active development, feature-complete for the core knowledge-management and chat workflows, with strong PWA and offline-sync capabilities.

## Stack

- **Language**: TypeScript 5.5.3
- **Framework**: React 18.3.1 (with React Router 6.26.2)
- **Runtime**: Browser (Vite 5.4.1 + @vitejs/plugin-react-swc; Node ‚â• 18 for tooling)
- **Database**: PostgreSQL via Supabase (with Supabase Auth, Edge Functions, and Row-Level Security)
- **Auth**: Supabase Auth ‚Äî email/password + TOTP MFA; RBAC via `user_roles` table; `ProtectedRoute` / `AdminRoute` guards
- **Testing**: Vitest 4.0.18 (unit + integration, jsdom); Playwright 1.57.0 (E2E)
- **CI/CD**: GitHub Actions (11 workflows: ci.yml, test.yml, release.yml, codeql.yml, deploy.yml, branch-cleanup.yml, and more)

## Architecture

```
Browser (React SPA)
  ‚îî‚îÄ React Query (TanStack) ‚Äî server state, caching
  ‚îî‚îÄ Context providers ‚Äî Auth, Offline, Theme, Confirmation
  ‚îî‚îÄ Service Worker (vite-plugin-pwa) ‚Äî offline cache + background sync queue
        ‚îÇ
        ‚ñº
  Service layer (src/services/)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ BaseService ‚Äî retry logic, error handling, auth validation
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ KnowledgeService ‚Äî CRUD + soft-delete + bulk operations
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ChatService ‚Äî TESSA conversation management
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ SearchService ‚Äî full-text + semantic search
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ NotificationService ‚Äî real-time notifications
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ UserService ‚Äî profile + preferences
  ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ AdminService + AuditService ‚Äî admin dashboard, audit logs
        ‚îÇ
        ‚ñº
  Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Edge Functions)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Auth ‚Äî session management, TOTP MFA, account lockout
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ RLS policies ‚Äî row-level security per user
  ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ Edge Functions (Deno)
      ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ chat-with-tessa-secure ‚Äî OpenAI proxy
      ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ parse-pdf / parse-docx / parse-xlsx ‚Äî document parsing
      ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ google-drive-oauth ‚Äî OAuth2 integration
      ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ send-backup-email ‚Äî Resend email export
      ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ security-headers ‚Äî CSP/HSTS injection
      ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ip-geolocation ‚Äî rate-limit geo-lookup
      ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ account-lockout ‚Äî failed-login tracking
      ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ system-status ‚Äî health checks
```

Background jobs: service worker background sync queue flushes pending writes when connectivity is restored. No server-side cron jobs in the frontend codebase.

## Key Directories

```
cortex-second-brain-4589/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ src/

‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ components/           # UI components (admin, auth, knowledge, tessa, etc.)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ config/               # Env-driven app config (ApiConfig, CacheConfig, FeatureFlags‚Ä¶)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ constants/            # Error messages, route names, other constants
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ contexts/             # React contexts (AuthContext, OfflineContext, ThemeContext)

‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ integrations/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ supabase/         # Auto-generated Supabase client + Database types
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ lib/                  # Core utilities (error-handling, api-utils, offline-storage)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ pages/                # Route-level page components (lazy-loaded)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ services/             # Service classes ‚Äî data access layer
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ types/                # Shared TypeScript types (index.ts re-exports all)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ utils/                # Pure utility functions (exportUtils, security, crypto, chatUtils)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ supabase/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ functions/            # Deno edge functions (one folder per function)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ migrations/           # PostgreSQL migration files (Supabase CLI)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ e2e/                      # Playwright end-to-end tests
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ docs/                     # Architecture, API, security, runbook, ADRs
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ public/                   # Static assets, PWA icons, manifest
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .github/workflows/        # 11 CI/CD workflow files
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ scripts/                  # Build/maintenance helper scripts
```

## Environment Variables

All variables are prefixed `VITE_` and exposed to the browser via Vite. **Never commit values.**

### Required

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` | Supabase project URL |
| `VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` | Supabase anon/public key |
| `VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID` | Supabase project reference ID |

### API & Retry

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `VITE_MAX_RETRIES` | `3` | Max retry attempts for failed requests |
| `VITE_RETRY_DELAY` | `1000` | Base retry delay (ms) |
| `VITE_MAX_RETRY_DELAY` | `10000` | Max retry delay (ms) |
| `VITE_API_TIMEOUT` | `30000` | Request timeout (ms) |
| `VITE_BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER` | `2` | Exponential backoff multiplier |

### Caching

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `VITE_CACHE_TTL` | `600000` | Cache item TTL (ms) |
| `VITE_MAX_CACHE_SIZE` | `1000` | Max cached items |
| `VITE_SW_CACHE_ENABLED` | `true` | Enable service worker cache |

### Rate Limiting

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `VITE_RATE_LIMIT_MAX` | `60` | Max requests per window |
| `VITE_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW` | `60000` | Rate-limit window (ms) |

### Feature Flags

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `VITE_PREFETCH_ENABLED` | `true` | Enable data prefetching |
| `VITE_OFFLINE_MODE` | `true` | Enable offline support |
| `VITE_PERF_MONITORING` | `true` | Enable Web Vitals monitoring |
| `VITE_DEBUG` | `true` (dev only) | Enable debug logging |
| `VITE_VIRTUAL_SCROLL` | `true` | Enable virtual scrolling |

### UI

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `VITE_DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE` | `50` | Default pagination size |
| `VITE_MAX_PAGE_SIZE` | `200` | Maximum pagination size |
| `VITE_SEARCH_DEBOUNCE` | `300` | Search input debounce (ms) |
| `VITE_TOAST_DURATION` | `5000` | Toast notification duration (ms) |

### Background Sync

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `VITE_SYNC_MAX_RETRIES` | `5` | Max sync retry attempts |
| `VITE_SYNC_RETRY_DELAY` | `1000` | Sync retry base delay (ms) |
| `VITE_SYNC_MAX_DELAY` | `300000` | Sync max delay (ms) |
| `VITE_SYNC_DEBOUNCE` | `500` | Sync queue debounce (ms) |

### Batch Operations

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `VITE_BATCH_SIZE` | `50` | Default bulk operation batch size |
| `VITE_MAX_BATCH_SIZE` | `200` | Maximum batch size |
| `VITE_BATCH_DELAY` | `100` | Delay between batches (ms) |

### External APIs

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `VITE_OPENAI_TIMEOUT` | `60000` | OpenAI call timeout (ms) |
| `VITE_GEO_TIMEOUT` | `5000` | Geolocation API timeout (ms) |

> **Edge Function secrets** (set via `supabase secrets set`, never in `.env`):
> `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `RESEND_API_KEY`

## Development Commands

```bash
# Install
npm install --legacy-peer-deps

# Run dev server
npm run dev

# Run tests (single run)
npm run test

# Run tests in watch mode
npm run test:watch

# Run tests with coverage report
npm run test:coverage

# Run E2E tests (requires built app + Playwright browsers)
npx playwright test

# Lint
npm run lint

# Lint with auto-fix
npm run lint:fix

# TypeScript type-check (no emit)
npm run type-check

# Build (production)
npm run build

# Build (development mode)
npm run build:dev

# Preview production build locally
npm run preview
```

## Conventions

- **File naming**: kebab-case for non-hook files (e.g., `knowledge.service.ts`, `error-handling.ts`); camelCase for hook files (e.g., `useKnowledge.ts`, `useRecaptcha.ts`); PascalCase for React component files (e.g., `StatusIndicator.tsx`, `PageTransition.tsx`)
- **Component structure**: One component per file; co-located `__tests__/` directories inside the component/hook folder
- **Service pattern**: Class-based services extending `BaseService`; exported as singleton (e.g., `export const KnowledgeService = new KnowledgeServiceImpl()`)
- **Hook pattern**: `useState` + `useEffect` for domain hooks (`useKnowledge`, `useChat`); TanStack Query `useQuery`/`useMutation` for infrastructure hooks (`useNotifications`, `useDashboardData`)
- **API patterns**: REST via Supabase PostgREST client; Supabase Edge Functions for AI/external API calls
- **Error handling**: `AppError` interface + `ApplicationError` class + `ErrorCode` enum + `createAppError()` factory in `src/lib/error-handling.ts`; all service errors thrown as `ApplicationError`; silent `catch {}` blocks are prohibited
- **Async pattern**: `async/await` throughout; no raw `.then()/.catch()` chains in new code
- **Type imports**: Use `import type { ‚Ä¶ }` for type-only imports (`@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-imports` enforced)
- **Path alias**: `@/` maps to `src/` ‚Äî always use this alias instead of relative paths crossing directories
- **Documentation files**: Named in `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.md` inside `docs/`

## Security Notes

- **Auth model**: Supabase Auth (email/password + optional TOTP MFA). Sessions stored in `localStorage` with auto-refresh. RBAC via `user_roles` table; admin access gated by `AdminRoute` component.
- **Secrets**: All via env vars only ‚Äî never commit credentials. Edge function secrets managed via `supabase secrets set`.
- **Input sanitization**: DOMPurify (`dompurify@3.2.6`) used for all user-generated HTML content (`src/utils/security.ts`)
- **Content Security Policy**: Injected by `security-headers` edge function and enforced by `SecurityHeaders` component
- **Account protection**: `account-lockout` edge function tracks failed login attempts; `ip-geolocation` used for rate limiting
- **Row-Level Security**: All Supabase tables have RLS policies ‚Äî users can only access their own data
- **No floating promises**: `@typescript-eslint/no-floating-promises` enforced as a warning; critical async calls must be awaited or `.catch()`-handled

## Known Issues / TODOs

No open `TODO` or `FIXME` comments exist in `src/` as of the last audit. Refer to `docs/ROADMAP.md` for planned features and `PRODUCT_AUDIT_AND_ROADMAP.md` for the backlog.

Pre-existing lint warnings (not errors introduced by this sprint):
- 2,400+ `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-*` warnings in the codebase ‚Äî these are set to `warn` intentionally to allow incremental adoption
- Supabase function files (`supabase/functions/**`) and `tailwind.config.ts` / `vitest.setup.ts` produce `parserOptions.project` parsing errors in ESLint because they are outside the TypeScript project roots ‚Äî these are pre-existing and not blocking

## Agent Instructions (for Copilot / Claude Code)

- Always run `npm run lint` before committing
- Always run `npm run test` before marking a task complete
- Always run `npm run type-check` to verify TypeScript compiles
- Do **NOT** modify: `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts` (auto-generated), `supabase/migrations/` (schema migrations)
- Do **NOT** alter database schema or migration files
- Do **NOT** change public API signatures, exported function names, or route paths during refactoring
- Match patterns from: `src/services/knowledge.service.ts` (service pattern), `src/hooks/useKnowledge.ts` (hook pattern), `src/lib/error-handling.ts` (error handling)
- New dependencies require explicit user approval ‚Äî use what is already installed
- All secrets via env vars only ‚Äî `VITE_*` for browser, Supabase secrets for edge functions
- Use `@/` path alias instead of relative imports across directories
- Use `import type { ‚Ä¶ }` for type-only imports
- Install with `npm install --legacy-peer-deps` (peer dep conflict between vitest versions)
